trends.name: Iran-US Escalation & Energy Crisis
trends.summary: Real-time escalation: Iran threatens to strike US company facilities and target Ukrainian infrastructure; Kuwait reports 7 hostile drones with 3 destroyed; threats to close Hormuz Strait and target energy facilities. This directly impacts oil prices, regional stability, and energy sector valuations amid SPR emergency release announcements.

trends.name: Oil Price Shock & SPR Strategic Reserve Release
trends.summary: US beginning largest-ever 86-million-barrel SPR dump via exchange program next week to combat oil surge from Iran war escalation. Market debate on whether crude can sustain $150+ amid emergency stockpile release; shipping and energy sector exposure critical for portfolio positioning.

trends.name: Middle East Peace Talks & Cease-Fire Momentum
trends.summary: Direct Lebanon-Israel talks expected in coming days led by Jared Kushner in Cyprus or Paris; Turkish FM urging return to negotiations. De-escalation talks create volatility hedge against further regional conflict; potential catalyst for oil pullback and risk-on sentiment reversal.

trends.name: Bitcoin Outperforming During Geopolitical Stress
trends.summary: Bitcoin has outpaced S&P 500, Nasdaq, and gold since Iran war escalation began, signaling flight-to-alternative-assets sentiment. Investors rotating into crypto as hedge against geopolitical risk and potential currency debasement; notable shift in asset allocation preferences.

trends.name: Meta Mass Layoffs (20%+) & Tech Workforce Contraction
trends.summary: Meta planning layoffs affecting 20% or more of workforce; signals accelerating AI/automation displacement across tech sector. Broader concern for labor markets, consumer spending, and macro slowdown; foreshadows similar moves across Big Tech creating structural employment headwinds.

trends.name: FOMC Meeting & Earnings Season Catalyst Week
trends.summary: FOMC meeting takes center stage alongside major earnings reports from Micron and Alibaba. Fed policy clarity combined with earnings surprises will determine whether market can sustain current valuations amid geopolitical headwinds; critical inflection point for rate expectations.

trends.name: Portfolio Rotation Away from Tech to Defensive/Income
trends.summary: Market rotation accelerating toward 7-12% yielding dividend stocks and income strategies; non-tech S&P 500 outperforming this year. Investors seeking stress-free income and defensive positioning amid elevated geopolitical risk and volatile equity markets; sector leadership shift underway.

trends.name: Caesars-Fertitta Deal & Hospitality M&A Activity
trends.summary: Fertitta in weekend deal talks to acquire Caesars while Carl Icahn watches from sidelines; signals consolidation in gaming/hospitality sector. Broader implications for leisure travel demand amid geopolitical uncertainty and potential recession concerns; activist investor positioning.

trends.name: Valuation Compression & P/E Ratio Contraction Risk
trends.summary: S&P 500 P/E at 21x with potential to slip into teens if bearish momentum persists despite rising EPS; market pricing in slowdown. Tension between earnings growth and multiple compression; investors hedging against 15-17x valuation environment amid rate uncertainty.

trends.name: Great Wealth Transfer & Inheritance Planning Surge
trends.summary: Older women poised to inherit majority of $54 trillion in great wealth transfer to widowed spouses; estate planning and trust structures in focus. Massive implications for asset management industry, tax planning, and capital reallocation; intergenerational wealth concentration reshaping financial services demand.

trends.name: Retirement Readiness Crisis & 401(k) Withdrawal Panic
trends.summary: Growing evidence that higher 401(k) withdrawal rates reflect desperation rather than recklessness; retirement adequacy crisis emerging. Despite $3M savings, 68-year-olds report unpreparedness; Social Security uncertainty and healthcare costs (Medicare Advantage reality check) driving withdrawal pressure on investment returns.

trends.name: Rare Undervalued Stock Opportunities Before Index Fund Surge
trends.summary: Market opportunity window identified before major passive inflows; portfolio of hated stocks crushing benchmarks in 2026. Tactical timing advantage for active managers capturing dislocated valuations before Wall Street index funds rebalance; specific plays in undervalued sectors (defense, energy, selectivity).
